problem: What triggers cancer cells to leave primary tumor and colonize new tissue?
mechanism: Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations
hypothesis: Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations may resolve What triggers cancer cells to leave primary tumor and colonize new tissue? by activating at the molecular level.

experiment.protocol: Mouse model knockout study with 35 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: What we observe is not nature itself but nature exposed to our method of questioning. — Heisenberg
